column1: # OWASP ZAP baseline rule overrides for Signacare EMR.
# ACTION is one of: IGNORE | WARN | FAIL
# Every override MUST have a REASON explaining why the rule is downgraded.
# Never add an IGNORE without a reference to the control that mitigates the
# finding elsewhere (e.g. "set by nginx, not the app").
# This file is consumed by scripts/zap/zap-baseline.sh and by the

column4: Cache-control headers are set per-route by CDN rules (feat s2.6); passive baseline cannot see them.
X-Frame-Options is set by Helmet at the app layer; baseline scanner sometimes misses it on streaming endpoints (/api/v1/events/stream).
CSP header is delivered by nginx in production — passive scan runs against the app-origin proxy so it may report missing header.
Absence of anti-CSRF tokens on GET endpoints is expected — CSRF is enforced on mutations only via X-CSRF-Token middleware.
